spec_match.rationale: Each preset and the custom-input path calls CSS.supports('at-rule(@x)') exactly as the spec defines, and the snippet generator emits valid `@supports at-rule(...)` wrappers.

interactive.rationale: Seven preset cards each with their own Check button, a custom-input probe, a checkbox grid that drives a snippet generator, and a live CSS editor that injects into a <style> tag — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises preset gallery with check buttons, snippet generator, and live CSS editor — page delivers each piece.

edge_cases.rationale: Probe wraps CSS.supports in try/catch (good for older browsers). The live CSS editor injects whatever the user types — a stray `}` could break parsing of subsequent rules in the same <style> tag.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colour palette, preset cards and badges follow the pattern.

openQuestions.title: Sandbox the live CSS editor
openQuestions.detail: Injecting raw CSS into a shared <style> tag means a typo (an extra `}`, a `/*` without `*/`) breaks parsing for everything downstream. Wrap each injection in its own <style> element that gets removed before the next attempt, so each test is isolated.

openQuestions.title: Show the browser-compat baseline table
openQuestions.detail: The page already has a COMPAT array with chrome/firefox/safari version-strings; surface this as a small browser-matrix table per preset so visitors learn not just 'does my browser support it' but 'what's the support floor across vendors'. The data is already in JS, just not rendered.

openQuestions.title: Pre-fill snippet bodies with worked examples
openQuestions.detail: The snippet generator emits empty `@supports at-rule(@x) { ... }` wrappers. For each preset, pre-fill a one-line example body (e.g. `@starting-style { from { opacity: 0; } }`) so the snippet is copy-pasteable into a real stylesheet.

openQuestions.title: Make the custom-input survive page reloads
openQuestions.detail: A small localStorage round-trip on the custom-probe field would let visitors build up a personal set of at-rules they care about and return to them later. Low-cost, high-value for the page being usable as a tool.

summary: Most tool-shaped of the five concepts — probe + snippet generator + live CSS injector in one page. Sandbox the live CSS editor per-injection and render the existing COMPAT data as a per-preset browser-version table.
